name: Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor gravity
summary: Replaces Newton's constant G by the inverse of a dynamical scalar field phi, giving a scalar-tensor theory of gravity inspired by Mach's principle. The single dimensionless parameter omega controls the deviation from GR; observational tests (Cassini Shapiro delay, lunar laser ranging) push omega > ~40000, recovering GR very closely. Foundational for all subsequent scalar-tensor gravity, Horndeski, and beyond.

addresses_puzzles.note: Generalisations (Brans-Dicke + potential, scalar-tensor cosmology) accommodate dark energy via slow-roll of phi; specific Lambda value not derived. Modern late-time-acceleration scalar-tensor theories descend from Brans-Dicke.

touches_benchmarks.note: Cassini Shapiro delay, LLR, binary-pulsar PPK tests bound omega > 40000 (PPN gamma - 1 < 2.3e-5), forcing the theory into the GR-like regime.
Pure Brans-Dicke has c_GW = c by construction (only the scalar mode propagates differently); graviton speed bound respected automatically.

introduces.note: Single dimensionless parameter omega (with omega -> infty recovering GR).

history: Brans and Dicke (1961), 'Mach's principle and a relativistic theory of gravitation', Phys. Rev. 124, 925. Foundational scalar-tensor theory; superseded for late-time cosmology by Horndeski and beyond-Horndeski theories, but observationally still constrained as a benchmark alternative to GR. Cassini 2003 (Bertotti-Iess-Tortora) gave the tightest omega > 4e4 bound.

references.citation: Brans and Dicke, 'Mach's principle and a relativistic theory of gravitation', Phys. Rev. 124, 925 (1961)
